metric_name: Ingres user satisfaction vs. Sybase user satisfaction
metric_value: Ingres users extremely satisfied; many Sybase customers would prefer Ingres; no Ingres users reported reliability problems
notes: Survey of 10 companies; Ingres: zero reliability complaints; Sybase: universal (mild) reliability issues

metric_value: Sybase pro-actively supports only Sun Microsystems and PS/2-OS/2; lacks resources to support Bull's wide family of platforms
notes: Sybase support staff advised customers to develop on Sun first then port; this created platform lock-in concerns

metric_value: Almost every contact volunteered rejection of Oracle due to basic architectural weaknesses for high-performance OLTP
notes: Oracle not directly surveyed; volunteered by contacts as rejected alternative

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends Ingres as preferred supplier; Ingres 6.3 earliest version Bull should select; Sybase only if marketing capability is priority
notes: Bull seeking common state-of-science RDBMS across all customer platform choices

metric_value: Bull should establish Application Implementation Business Center for RDBMS adoption support; revenue from training, design, implementation, upgrade support, SI
notes: Aberdeen: neither Ingres nor Sybase has resources to support application development; customers use third-party consultants

metric_value: Year 0-1: evaluate; Year 1-2: develop pilot with consultants and train staff; Year 2-3: implement pilot and continue development
notes: Minimum 2 years to be highly accurate in describing product capability and limitations

metric_name: Production state of Ingres v6 and Sybase v4 in 1990
metric_value: Both Ingres v6 and Sybase v4 applications are just now going into production or within next 3 months; high-performance OLTP not yet proved at scale
notes: Most production users still running Ingres v5 and Sybase v3 for decision support

metric_value: Sybase was first RDBMS supplier to show vision of high-performance OLTP and tailor products to win customers; Ingres consistently poorer marketer
notes: Ingres does not provide customers with stream of marketing materials needed to justify choice to executive management

metric_value: Trade press reported Salomon moving IMS to Sybase; Aberdeen interview revealed Salomon wished it had chosen Ingres instead
notes: Salomon acquired Sybase for DB2 gateway and Pyramid support ahead of normal schedule

metric_name: Mainframe OLTP performance gap with RDBMS
metric_value: Even most advanced RDBMS users not achieving mainframe-level performance; high-performance OLTP migration confidence high for near term
notes: Customers confident technology will provide capability; no intent to return to flat-file or hierarchical systems

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts Ingres 6.3 as reliable, stable preferred supplier for Bull; Ingres customers show higher loyalty than Sybase
notes: Prediction that Ingres would outperform Sybase long-term

metric_value: Ingres sold to ASK Group (1990), CA (1994), spun out (2005), became Actian (2011); acquired by HCL for $330M (2018); Ingres 12.0 active in 2024
notes: Ingres never dominated market but survived; Oracle won the RDBMS market

metric_value: SAP acquired Sybase for $5.8 billion (July 2010); became SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise
notes: Aberdeen correctly identified Sybase's platform limitations and reliability disadvantages in 1990

metric_name: Ingres CASE tools and Knowledge Manager satisfaction
metric_value: Ingres version 6 provides all growth path customers can implement today (especially with Knowledge Manager); Cadre/Ingres CASE tools anticipated
notes: Strong Ingres v6 loyalty; users looking forward to CASE tools integration

metric_name: SQL standardization impact on RDBMS adoption
metric_value: Need for user staff to understand SQL/relational model is major adoption barrier; outside consulting needed for all surveyed companies
notes: ANSI SQL not yet ubiquitous; staff training was 1-3 year investment

metric_value: Oracle became world's largest RDBMS vendor; Oracle Database market share ~28% of RDBMS market in 2024
notes: Despite 1990 survey showing universal rejection for OLTP, Oracle recovered and dominated through aggressive marketing and performance improvements

metric_value: Sun Microsystems acquired by Oracle Corporation for $7.4B in 2010
notes: Ironic that Oracle acquired the company that hosted Sybase's primary development platform

metric_name: Hardware platform importance in RDBMS acquisition
metric_value: No hardware manufacturer was part of acquisition decision making process for surveyed customers; RDBMS selected independently of hardware
notes: This validated Aberdeen's advice that Bull's value was in implementation support rather than hardware bundling

metric_name: Ingres version 5 vs. version 6 satisfaction gap
metric_value: Ingres users believe deficiencies in version 5 corrected in version 6; v6 gained tremendous additional customer satisfaction and loyalty
notes: Major product improvement between v5 and v6 validated Aberdeen's recommendation of v6.3 minimum

metric_name: Both Ingres and Sybase resource limitations
metric_value: All 10 customers believed their supplier did not have resources for envisioned product functionality and support services
notes: This universal finding drove Aberdeen's recommendation for Bull's Application Implementation Business Center
